title: Circuitry for reducing leakage current in configuration memory
abstract: Integrated circuits may include dual mode memory cells. Dual mode memory cells may be operated in a lookup-table mode or a memory mode. A dual mode memory cell may have configuration ports for supporting a configuration operation and user ports for supporting a user mode operation. When performing configuration operations in the memory mode, the configuration ports may be gated off to prevent existing user data from being accessed. Each column of memory cells may be arranged into groups. Each group of memory cells in a column may be connected to a respective local data line, which is connected to a global data line via a switch. The switch may be selectively activated to short the local data line to the global data line. Configured in this hierarchical data line architecture, leakage at the global data line can dramatically be reduced, and the memory cell read margin is improved.
claims:
Claims What is Claimed is: 1. An integrated circuit, comprising: a first dual mode memory cell; a second dual mode memory cell; a local data line that is connected to the first dual mode memory cell and the second dual mode memory cell; a global data line; and a circuit that selectively couples the local data line to the global data line. 2. The integrated circuit of claim 1, wherein the circuit comprises a pass gate transistor. 3. The integrated circuit of claim 1, wherein the circuit comprises a bidirectional tristate buffer. 4. The integrated circuit of claim 1, wherein the first and second dual mode memory cells are operable in a configuration mode and a user memory mode. 5. The integrated circuit of claim 4, wherein the first and second dual mode memory cells each have a configuration port and a user port, the integrated circuit further comprising: gating logic at the configuration port, wherein the configuration port is gated off by the gating logic while the first and second dual mode memory cells are operated in the user memory mode. 6. The integrated circuit of claim 5, wherein the first and second dual mode memory cells are powered using a memory cell supply voltage and a ground power supply voltage, and wherein the configuration port is addressed using signals that swing between the ground power supply voltage and an overdrive voltage that is greater than the memory cell supply voltage. 7. The integrated circuit of claim 5, wherein the gating logic comprises a logic AND gate. 8. The integrated circuit of any one of claims 1-7, further comprising: a third dual mode memory cell; a fourth dual mode memory cell; an additional local data line that is connected to the third dual mode memory cell and the fourth dual mode memory cell; and an additional circuit that selectively couples the additional local data line to the global data line . 9. A method for operating an integrated circuit, comprising: operating a plurality of dual mode memory cells in a first mode; operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in a second mode that is different than the first mode, wherein the plurality of dual mode memory cells is connected to a local data line; and 

 with a circuit, selectively coupling the local data line to a global data line . 10. The method of claim 9, wherein operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in the first mode comprises operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in a memory mode. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in the second mode comprises operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in a lookup-table (LUT) mode. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein each dual mode memory cell in the plurality of dual mode memory cells has a user port for supporting a user mode operation and a configuration port for supporting a configuration operation, the method further comprising: not gating the configuration port during the LUT mode. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein each dual mode memory cell in the plurality of dual mode memory cells has a user port for supporting a user mode operation and a configuration port for supporting a configuration operation, the method further comprising: gating the configuration port during the memory mode . 14. The method of claim 13, wherein gating the configuration port comprises outputting an address signal 

at a ground power supply voltage level to at least one of the plurality of dual mode memory cells. 15. The method of any one of claims 9-14, further comprising: with a control block, selectively outputting an asserted control signal to activate the circuit . 16. An integrated circuit, comprising: an array of multimode memory cells, wherein : a column of multimode memory cells in the array is organized into at least first and second groups of multimode memory cells; the first group of multimode memory cells is directly connected to a first local data line; the second group of multimode memory cells is directly connected to a second local data line; the first local data line is connected to a global data line via a first circuit; and the second local data line is connected to the global data line via a second circuit. 17. The integrated circuit of claim 16, wherein each multimode memory cell in the array is operable in at least a memory mode and a lookup-table mode. 18. The integrated circuit of claim 16, wherein the first circuit comprises an n-channel transistor. 19. The integrated circuit of any one of claims 16-19, wherein the first circuit comprises a bidirectional buffer . 20. The integrated circuit of any one of claims 16-19, wherein the first circuit comprises a tristate buffer . 21. An integrated circuit, comprising: means for operating a plurality of dual mode memory cells in a first mode and in a second mode that is different than the first mode, wherein the plurality of dual mode memory cells is connected to a local data line; and means for selectively coupling the local data line to a global data line. 21. The integrated circuit of claim 21, wherein the means operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in the first mode comprises means for operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in a memory mode. 22. The integrated circuit of claim 21, wherein the means for operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in the second mode comprises means for operating the plurality of dual mode memory cells in a lookup-table (LUT) mode. 23. The integrated circuit of claim 22, wherein each dual mode memory cell in the plurality of dual mode memory cells has a user port for supporting a user mode 

operation and a configuration port for supporting a configuration operation, further comprising: means for gating the configuration port during the memory mode. 24. The integrated circuit of claim 23, wherein the means for gating the configuration port comprises means for outputting an address signal at a ground power supply voltage level to at least one of the plurality of dual mode memory cells. 25. The integrated circuit of any one of claims 21-24, further comprising: means for selectively outputting an asserted control signal to activate the circuit.
